entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop (Sorghum bicolor) whose productivity is analyzed in rainy seasons

entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A grain legume (Cajanus cajan) used in intercropping and rotation systems

entities.name: COWPEA
entities.description: A grain legume used in intercropping systems

entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A grain legume (Cicer arietinum) used in rotation systems

entities.name: SAFFLOWER
entities.description: A non-legume crop (Carthamus tinctorius) used in a comparison system

entities.name: LEGUMES
entities.description: A group of nitrogen-fixing crops used to benefit succeeding crops

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area

entities.name: SOIL TOTAL NITROGEN
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen present in the soil

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops simultaneously on the same field

entities.name: SEQUENTIAL CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing two or more crops in sequence on the same field

entities.name: VERTISOL MANAGEMENT TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: Techniques used to manage Vertisols for consecutive cropping

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of growing different crops in succession on the same land

entities.name: LONG-TERM TRIAL
entities.description: An extended research study established in 1983 to intensify cropping

entities.name: VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of clay-rich soil found in southern and central India

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period of high rainfall affecting land management and crop growth

entities.name: POST-RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The period following the rainy season used for cropping

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICAL
entities.description: The climatic zone characterizing the study area

entities.name: SOIL-BORNE FUNGI
entities.description: Fungi living in the soil that cause yield decline in pigeonpea

entities.name: NEMATODES
entities.description: Microscopic worms in the soil that cause yield decline in pigeonpea

entities.name: NITROGEN
entities.description: A critical plant nutrient provided by legumes or fertilizers

entities.name: NON-LEGUME SYSTEM
entities.description: A cropping system consisting of sorghum and safflower without legumes

entities.name: SOUTHERN AND CENTRAL INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region where the study was conducted

relationships.description: provides nitrogen benefit to succeeding crops
provides less marked benefits than pigeonpea
causes a 12-year build up of soil total N
helps overcome soil-borne fungi and nematode problems
is the location where Vertisols are cropped
